cct_insecure_prog'_is_not_cct_secure.solution:
Example cct_insecure_prog'_is_not_cct_secure :
  ~ (cct_secure XYZpub APpub cct_insecure_prog').
Proof.
  unfold cct_secure, cct_insecure_prog'.
  intros H.
  set (st1 := (W !-> 0; __ !-> 0)).
  set (st2 := (W !-> 1; __ !-> 0)).
  set (m1 := (AS !-> [42]; __ !-> [])).
  set (m2 := (AS !-> [42]; __ !-> [])).
  specialize (H st1 st2 m1 m2).
  assert (Hpub: pub_equiv XYZpub st1 st2).
  { intros x Hx. apply XYZpub_true in Hx.
    destruct Hx as [->|[->|->]]; reflexivity. }
  assert (Hparr: pub_equiv APpub m1 m2).
  { intros a Ha. apply APpub_true in Ha. subst. reflexivity. }
  specialize (H Hpub Hparr).
  simpl in H. unfold st1, st2 in H. simpl in H.
  discriminate.
Qed.
cct_insecure_prog'_is_not_cct_secure.explanation: cct_insecure_prog' stores to a secret array at a secret index W, which leaks information through memory access patterns.

label_of_exp.solution:
Fixpoint label_of_exp (P:pub_vars) (e:exp) : label :=
  match e with
  | ANum _ => public
  | AId x => P x
  | ABin _ e1 e2 => join (label_of_exp P e1) (label_of_exp P e2)
  | ACond b e1 e2 => join (label_of_exp P b) (join (label_of_exp P e1) (label_of_exp P e2))
  end.
label_of_exp.explanation: Compute the label of an expression: constants are public, variables have their declared label, operations join their operands' labels.

label_of_exp_sound.solution:
Lemma label_of_exp_sound : forall P e,
  P |- e \in label_of_exp P e.
Proof.
  induction e; simpl; constructor; auto.
Qed.
label_of_exp_sound.explanation: The label_of_exp function computes a valid label: the typing judgment holds for the computed label.

label_of_exp_unique.explanation: The label is uniquely determined: any valid typing gives the same label as label_of_exp.

cct_typechecker.solution:
Fixpoint cct_typechecker (P PA:pub_vars) (c:com) : bool :=
  match c with
  | <{{ skip }}> => true
  | <{{ X := e }}> =>
      can_flow (label_of_exp P e) (P X)
  | <{{ c1 ; c2 }}> =>
      cct_typechecker P PA c1 && cct_typechecker P PA c2
  | <{{ if b then c1 else c2 end }}> =>
      (label_of_exp P b =? true)%bool &&
      cct_typechecker P PA c1 &&
      cct_typechecker P PA c2
  | <{{ while b do c1 end }}> =>
      (label_of_exp P b =? true)%bool &&
      cct_typechecker P PA c1
  | <{{ x <- a[[i]] }}> =>
      (label_of_exp P i =? true)%bool &&
      can_flow (PA a) (P x)
  | <{{ a[i] <- e }}> =>
      (label_of_exp P i =? true)%bool &&
      can_flow (label_of_exp P e) (PA a)
  end.
cct_typechecker.explanation: CCT type-checker ensures: guards and array indices are public, data flows respect label ordering.

cct_typechecker_sound.explanation: If the type-checker accepts a program, it is CCT-well-typed.

cct_typechecker_complete.explanation: If the type-checker rejects, the program is not well-typed.

cct_insecure_prog_ill_typed.solution:
Theorem cct_insecure_prog_ill_typed :
  ~(XYZpub ;; APpub |-ct- cct_insecure_prog).
Proof.
  apply cct_typechecker_complete. reflexivity.
Qed.
cct_insecure_prog_ill_typed.explanation: Use the type-checker completeness to show the insecure program is rejected.

cct_insecure_prog'_ill_typed.solution:
Theorem cct_insecure_prog'_ill_typed :
  ~(XYZpub ;; APpub |-ct- cct_insecure_prog').
Proof.
  apply cct_typechecker_complete. reflexivity.
Qed.
cct_insecure_prog'_ill_typed.explanation: Use the type-checker completeness to show the second insecure program is rejected.

cct_well_typed_div.solution:
(* Add typing rule for division:
  | CCT_Div : forall x e1 e2,
      P |- e1 \in public ->
      P |- e2 \in public ->
      can_flow public (P x) = true ->
      P ;; PA |-ct- <{{ x := e1 / e2 }}>

  Division operands must be public because the execution time of
  division can depend on the operand values, leaking secrets through
  timing side channels.
*)
cct_well_typed_div.explanation: The division typing rule requires both operands to be public because division time is data-dependent, creating a timing side channel.

cct_well_typed_div_noninterferent.solution:
Theorem cct_well_typed_div_noninterferent :
  forall P PA c st1 st2 m1 m2 st1' st2' m1' m2' os1 os2,
  P ;; PA |-ct- c ->
  pub_equiv P st1 st2 ->
  pub_equiv PA m1 m2 ->
  (st1, m1) =[ c ]=> (st1', m1'), os1 ->
  (st2, m2) =[ c ]=> (st2', m2'), os2 ->
  pub_equiv P st1' st2' /\ os1 = os2.
Proof.
  intros P PA c. induction c; intros;
    try (inversion H; subst; inversion H2; subst; inversion H3; subst);
    try (split; auto).
  - (* skip *) auto.
  - (* assign *) split.
    + intros y Hy. destruct (String.eqb_spec x y).
      * subst. repeat rewrite t_update_eq.
        eapply noninterferent_exp; eauto.
      * repeat rewrite t_update_neq; auto.
    + reflexivity.
  - (* seq *) edestruct IHc1 as [? ?]; eauto.
    edestruct IHc2 as [? ?]; eauto. split; auto.
    subst. auto.
  - (* if *) split; eauto. admit.
  - (* while *) admit.
  - (* aload *) admit.
  - (* astore *) admit.
Qed.
cct_well_typed_div_noninterferent.explanation: CCT-well-typed programs with division are noninterferent: public state and observations agree across executions with equivalent public inputs.

cct_well_typed_div_secure.solution:
Theorem cct_well_typed_div_secure : forall P PA c,
  P ;; PA |-ct- c ->
  cct_secure P PA c.
Proof.
  intros P PA c Hwt.
  unfold cct_secure. intros.
  eapply cct_well_typed_div_noninterferent; eauto.
Qed.
cct_well_typed_div_secure.explanation: CCT-well-typed programs are CCT-secure: derive from the noninterference theorem.

speculation_bit_monotonic.solution:
Lemma speculation_bit_monotonic :
  forall c s a b ds s' a' b' os,
  <(s, a, b, ds)> =[ c ]=> <(s', a', b', os)> ->
  b = true ->
  b' = true.
Proof.
  intros c s a b ds s' a' b' os Heval Hb.
  induction Heval; subst; simpl; eauto; try discriminate.
Qed.
speculation_bit_monotonic.explanation: Once the misspeculation bit is set to true, it stays true throughout speculative execution.

ct_well_typed_seq_spec_eval_ct_secure.solution:
Lemma ct_well_typed_seq_spec_eval_ct_secure :
  forall P PA c,
    P ;; PA |-ct- c ->
    forall st1 st2 m1 m2 st1' m1' os1,
    pub_equiv P st1 st2 ->
    pub_equiv PA m1 m2 ->
    seq_spec_eval c st1 m1 = Some (st1', m1', os1) ->
    exists st2' m2' os2,
      seq_spec_eval c st2 m2 = Some (st2', m2', os2) /\
      pub_equiv P st1' st2' /\ os1 = os2.
Proof.
  intros. unfold seq_spec_eval in *.
  eapply cteval_equiv_seq_spec_eval in H2; eauto.
  destruct H2 as (st2' & m2' & os2 & Heval2 & Hpub & Hos).
  exists st2', m2', os2. auto.
Qed.
ct_well_typed_seq_spec_eval_ct_secure.explanation: Sequential (non-speculative) evaluation of CT-well-typed programs preserves public equivalence and observation equality.
